description:
Context:
        You are a DevSecOps engineer who must enforce an internal “policy-as-code” rule that every project’s live configuration files
        must be archived daily and the action must be written to an auditable log.  
        A small demo project has been placed at:

            /home/user/projects/myapp/config/

        The directory already contains three text configuration files ending in “.conf”.
        Your goal is to create a single compressed archive of ALL *.conf files that live directly inside that directory
        (do NOT recurse into sub-directories and do NOT include any non-*.conf files).
        When the archive is finished you must also append a one-line entry to a log file that will be used later for
        automated compliance checks.

    Required end-state (what automated tests will verify):
        1. A directory named “/home/user/backups” exists (create it if necessary; use default user permissions).
        2. Inside that directory there is exactly one new gzip-compressed tarball with the following strict naming pattern
           (tests are case-sensitive):
               myapp_conf_backup_YYYYMMDD.tar.gz
           where YYYYMMDD must match the current system date at the time the task is executed.
           • The tarball must contain only the three “.conf” files from  
             /home/user/projects/myapp/config/ and they must sit at the archive root
             (example listing after extraction: app.conf, db.conf, secrets.conf).
        3. Also inside “/home/user/backups” there is an audit log file named “backup_audit.log”.
           Append a new single-line record in the exact format shown below.  If the log file does not yet exist,
           create it before writing the line:

               YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS | OK | 3 files | /home/user/backups/myapp_conf_backup_YYYYMMDD.tar.gz

           • YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS is the current local time in 24-hour ISO-8601 format.
           • The literal string ‘OK’ must appear exactly as shown.
           • “3 files” reflects the number of files you placed in the archive; do not alter this number.
           • The last field is the absolute path to the archive produced in step 2.
           • Fields are separated by a single space, a pipe (“|”), and a single space.

    Scope & constraints:
        • Achieve the end-state using no more than three shell commands (a typical solution is: make directory,
          create tarball, echo a log line).
        • Do not use sudo or any elevated privileges.
        • Do not modify or move the original *.conf files.
        • Ensure the date strings in both filename and log line match exactly (tests compare them).

    Deliverable to the end user:
        No extra console output is required beyond what the individual commands normally show, but the filesystem must match
        everything described above; especially the archive name, its internal file list, and the correctly formatted audit log line.
truth:
# Pre-existing filesystem objects before the agent starts:
    Directory: /home/user/projects/myapp/config/
    Files in that directory (all readable):
        /home/user/projects/myapp/config/app.conf
            (content) APP_ENV=production
        /home/user/projects/myapp/config/db.conf
            (content) DB_HOST=localhost
        /home/user/projects/myapp/config/secrets.conf
            (content) API_KEY=demo123

    # What the automated tests will look for AFTER the agent finishes:
    1. The directory /home/user/backups must exist.
    2. Archive file path (example if run on 2024-08-15):
           /home/user/backups/myapp_conf_backup_20240815.tar.gz
       Tests will open the tarball and assert that:
           • It contains exactly three members: app.conf, db.conf, secrets.conf
           • No sub-directories, no leading “./”, and no additional files are present.

    3. Log file path:
           /home/user/backups/backup_audit.log
       Tests will check that the very last line of this file matches the regex  
           ^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2} \| OK \| 3 files \| /home/user/backups/myapp_conf_backup_\d{8}\.tar\.gz$
       and that the timestamp (first 19 characters) and the 8-digit date segment embedded in the filename are identical.

    # No other files or directories need to be created.
name: task_000000_f90376c0
